title: pi-0: A Vision-Language-Action Flow Model for General Robot Control
description: VLA using flow matching on pre-trained VLM; trained on 10K+ hrs, 7 platforms, 68 tasks; 50Hz dexterous control including laundry folding.
note: Open weights on HF/LeRobot; PI raised $400M+.

title: pi-0.5: A VLA Model with Open-World Generalization
description: First end-to-end VLA performing long-horizon tasks (cleaning kitchens/bedrooms) in entirely unseen homes. Defines current VLA generalization frontier.

title: GR00T N1: Open Foundation Model for Generalist Humanoid Robots
description: 2.2B-param open VLA with dual-system (VLM + DiT flow-matching); deployed on Fourier GR-1, 1X NEO. N1.6: 2x larger DiT. 60+ partners.
note: GTC announcement; open weights at NVIDIA/Isaac-GR00T.

title: OpenVLA: An Open-Source Vision-Language-Action Model
description: 7B open VLA outperforming RT-2-X (55B) by 16.5% with 7x fewer params; OFT achieves 97.1% LIBERO. Most-used open VLA baseline.

title: RDT-1B: A Diffusion Foundation Model for Bimanual Manipulation
description: ICLR 2025. 1.2B-param diffusion transformer for bimanual tasks; pre-trained on 1M+ episodes from 46 datasets; zero-shot + few-shot generalization. MIT licensed.

title: Training Agents Inside of Scalable World Models (Dreamer 4)
description: Nature 2025. First to obtain diamond in Minecraft from pure offline data (~0.7% success rate). Corrected: old '100%' was incorrect.

title: Gemini Robotics: Bringing AI into the Physical World
description: More than doubles generalization benchmarks vs other VLAs; thinking-before-acting + Motion Transfer; ER 1.5 SOTA on 15 embodied reasoning benchmarks.

title: A Careful Examination of Large Behavior Models (TRI LBM)
description: 1,800 blind A/B real-world trials: multi-task pretraining (1,700 hrs, 500+ tasks) makes policies more successful and robust; performance increases predictably with scale.
note: First truly rigorous real-world VLA evaluation.

title: Neural Scaling Laws in Robotics
description: Meta-analysis of 131 data-scaling studies: avg power law exponent -0.276 (much weaker than LLMs); naively pooling heterogeneous data can induce negative transfer.

title: Rethinking VLA Scaling
description: Unified EEF-relative action representation is critical; naive dataset pooling causes negative transfer; sensory dropout doesn't consistently help at scale.

title: VLA Vulnerability Analysis
description: VLAs extremely sensitive to camera viewpoint and initial state (95%->30% success); insensitive to language variations -- may ignore instructions entirely.

title: The Reality Gap in Robotics: Challenges, Solutions, and Best Practices
description: Zero-shot sim-to-real restricted to medium-complexity rigid-body tasks; for deformables, fluids, precision assembly: real data maintains irreducible premium value.

title: PACE: Systematic Sim-to-Real Transfer for Diverse Legged Robots
description: Physics-grounded energy model beats brute-force randomization; 32% reduction in Cost of Transport on ANYmal; validated on 10+ robots.

title: State of VLA Research at ICLR 2026
description: 164 VLA submissions; discrete diffusion VLAs emerging; LIBERO near-solved at 97%+; gap between frontier (pi-0, Gemini) and academic research remains large.

title: Evaluating pi-0 in the Wild
authors: Jie Wang et al. (UPenn GRASP Lab)
description: pi-0 achieves 42.3% avg task progress zero-shot -- unprecedented but reveals failures: no collision avoidance, fine-grained manipulation failures.

title: Self-Improving VLA with PLD
description: RL-generated data delivers >50% performance gains in SimplerEnv; >99% SR on Franka tasks; 1hr continuous GPU operation without human.

title: IntPhys2: Benchmarking Intuitive Physics Understanding
description: Current SOTA models ~50% (random level) in complex scenes; humans near-perfect. Jun 2025.

title: RoboCasa: Large-Scale Simulation of Everyday Tasks for Generalist Robots
description: 365 tasks, 2,500+ kitchens. ICLR 2026. VLA model evaluation benchmark.
